title: Error "file name too long" if DOCKER_CONFIG defined and path more 105 characters. Podman 3.0.1
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
If I use VOLUME instruction into Dockerfile and set env DOCKER_CONFIG with path length more 105 characters - I get error 'file name too long'.
It relate with authfile. But if I additionaly set REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE more 105 characters - it works without errors.

Also there are log spam like:
`time="2021-03-05T14:56:50Z" level=info msg="Using DOCKER_CONFIG environment variable for authfile path /tmp/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/123456789/12"`
But it looks like there was fixed:
Setting DOCKER_CONFIG leads to 20 line authfile log spam on INFO log level #9473



**Output of `podman version`:**
```
bash-4.2# podman version
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Fri Feb 19 20:35:26 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
bash-4.2# uname -a
Linux 04ee2482f4ee 5.9.3-1.el7.elrepo.x86_64 #1 SMP Sat Oct 31 09:41:18 EDT 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
**testcase:**
1. create Dockerfile
```
bash-4.2# echo '
> FROM centos:7.8.2003 as base
> RUN mkdir /myvol
> RUN echo "hello world" > /myvol/greeting
> VOLUME /myvol
> ' >  Dockerfile
bash-4.2# unset REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE
bash-4.2# unset DOCKER_CONFIG
```
2. run build with DOCKER_CONFIG  with path length 105 chars.
build OK
``` 
bash-4.2# export DOCKER_CONFIG=/tmp/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/123456789/1
bash-4.2# podman build -t test1 .
INFO[0000] Using DOCKER_CONFIG environment variable for authfile path /tmp/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/123456789/1

--> 2fc5fc8e06e
2fc5fc8e06e4c109d5bfef5bbb65272b11267c439e77a66d1ef222901aa657c7
```

4. set REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE with 106 path length
build OK
and there are no "info" spam
```
bash-4.2# export REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE=/tmp/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/123456789/12
bash-4.2# podman build -t test1 .
```


comments.body:
@sappersd: The label(s) `kind/error` cannot be applied, because the repository doesn't have them.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9634):

>/kind error
>
>If I use VOLUME instruction into Dockerfile and set env DOCKER_CONFIG with path length more 105 characters - I get error 'file name too long'.
>It relate with authfile. But if I additionaly set REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE more 105 characters - it works without errors.
>
>Also there are log spam like:
>`time="2021-03-05T14:56:50Z" level=info msg="Using DOCKER_CONFIG environment variable for authfile path /tmp/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/1234567890/123456789/12"`
>But it looks like there was fixed:
>Setting DOCKER_CONFIG leads to 20 line authfile log spam on INFO log level #9473
>
>
>
>**Output of `podman version`:**
>```
>bash-4.2# podman version
>Version:      3.0.1
>API Version:  3.0.0
>Go Version:   go1.15.5
>Built:        Fri Feb 19 20:35:26 2021
>OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
>bash-4.2# uname -a
>Linux 04ee2482f4ee 5.9.3-1.el7.elrepo.x86_64 #1 SMP Sat Oct 31 09:41:18 EDT 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
>```
>**testcase:**
>1. create Dockerfile
>```
>bash-4.2# echo '
>> FROM centos:7.8.2003 as base
>> RUN mkdir /myvol
>> RUN echo "hello world" > /myvol/greeting
>> VOLUME /myvol

>bash-4.2# unset REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE
>bash-4.2# unset DOCKER_CONFIG

>2. run build with DOCKER_CONFIG  with path length 105 chars.
>build OK
